title: Optimizing Memoized React Function Component Re-renders Due to Changing Deeply Nested Objects

statement.markdown: Given a memoized React function component `DeeplyNestedMemo`, which renders a deeply nested object `data` recursively, optimize the component to prevent unnecessary re-renders when the `data` object changes. The component should only re-render when the reference of the `data` object changes, not when its deeply nested properties change.
statement.inputFormat: An object `data` with deeply nested properties
statement.notes: The component should only re-render when the `data` object reference changes, not when its deeply nested properties change.

constraints.description: The number of nested levels in the `data` object
The maximum number of properties in each nested level

examples.explanation: The component should re-render only when the `data` object reference changes
The component should re-render only when the `data` object reference changes

hints.text: Use the `useMemo` hook to memoize the component's dependencies
Use a deep comparison function to check for reference changes in the `data` object

editorial.title: Optimizing Memoized React Function Component Re-renders Due to Changing Deeply Nested Objects
editorial.intuition: Memoize the component's dependencies and use a deep comparison function to check for reference changes in the `data` object
editorial.bruteForce: Use a recursive approach to check for changes in each nested level
editorial.betterSolution: Use the `useMemo` hook and a deep comparison function
editorial.optimalSolution: Use the `useMemo` hook and a deep comparison function with a custom comparison function
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The component should only re-render when the `data` object reference changes
commonMistakes: Not using a deep comparison function
